name: Better Planet Generation
summary: A huge planet generation overhaul that aims at making your hundreds of hours of exploration in the magnificent, infinite universe of No Man's Sky less repetitive and hopefully even more memorable.
author: WoodyMontana
description:
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][i][b]                                                     [size=6]Better Planet Generation[/size][/b][/i][/size]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629738968-1312445652.jpeg[/img]

[size=4][b]More planets

[/b][size=3]Better Planet Generation is a total overhaul of the planetary generation in No Man's Sky.[/size] [size=3]It improves by a significant margin the procedural aspect of the universe while at the same time respecting the original work and vision from Hello Games.[/size]

[size=3]It adds thousands of new distinct environments to the universe[/size][/size]. All of them come in five different life levels and varying placement scales and layouts. These environments were made to seamlessly combine with each other in many different ways.

Better Planet Generation is designed to bring variety to the different biomes you will visit but most of all it brings a lot of variation to the universe. Outside of the countless combinations of assets and their resulting new environments, planets you visit will present themselves differently each time. Placement layouts, scale of the regions, density, variety of assets and their respective scale, ...

No planet will look exactly the same. Unless, of course, it is a very simple one. More on that below.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1631005159-472402214.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936034-1789086190.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1610300150-1298994048.jpeg[/img]


[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=4][b]Sub Biomes[/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Caves and underwater sub biomes have also been entirely overhauled.

Tons of new variants have been added underground and just like on the surface, additional objects can spawn to increase variety even more.

﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410276-924973523.jpeg[/img]﻿

Underwater has been completely revamped and is now generated through a triple layered dynamic framework combining assets to generate highly varied and detailed environments.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629738939-165524032.jpeg[/img]
[b][size=4]
Unique planets

[/size][/b][size=4][size=3]On top of all the added variety, BPG also uses a flexible dynamic generation framework that can take many shapes[/size][/size] and create countless planetary variations, even when the same environments are being used multiple times.

Each planet generated has its own selection of parts based on its biome. Some liberties have been taken when designing and attributing those parts to the various biomes. Some objects have been injected to improve variety, but some restrictions have also been applied to preserve each biome's identity. Better Planet Generation also introduces countless restored, modified or repurposed assets to the game in order to increase the variety while preserving its visual style.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936068-132859967.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936034-754906087.jpeg[/img]

[/size][/size][/size][/size]
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]Natural Generation[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Better Planet Generation is a vastly different experience compared to the unmodded game and even other biome mods.
Not even taking into account the small details, it is more than likely that you will never be able to encounter all the possible assets combinations this mod can generate. Let alone in the different regional layouts each dynamic template can result in.

Each biome has access to multiple procedural templates. Each producing different results in terms of layouts and objects combinations.
Their common goal is to show you a different kind of environment each time. Aesthetically and structurally.
No more "postcard" planets where most of what there is to see is right around the corner. The scale of the generation can get extremely vast compared to the vanilla game.

Planetary generation can now affect your visual enjoyment and the gameplay.
It is a video game, after all.

A world generated at its fullest possible level and highest complexity can take you some time to fully explore on foot. Meaning seeing the most notable regions (and not all of them) and scanning everything.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630052272-1155900954.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1610300150-2086924238.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630272224-118529207.jpeg[/img]
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]More Variation[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

On the other hand, Better Planet Generation also allows more simple, generic yet still visually interesting planets to see :

- Planets only covered in grass and some plants, rocks or both.
- More barren worlds dominated by giant stone or crystal spires.
- Radioactive wastelands.
- Planets dotted with trees or occasional small forests.
- Volcanic wastelands.
- Toxic fields.
- Rocky swamps.
- ...

And the list goes on and on.
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3]The occurence of these "generic" planets only relies on probabilites. None of my templates is designed to specifically produce them.
I made sure they still looked good, though.[/size][/size][/size][/size]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410296-1590906166.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1626079315-1486130072.jpeg[/img]

[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]Different Experience[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Better Planet Generation is not for everyone. Planets can be both very detailed in every aspect or very simple.
If you expect to always see trees and a full grass coverage on your Lush planet or anywhere else, there are other mods for that.
This is not what you should expect using BPG.

Now, whether you are a new player ready to explore an infinite universe or a more weathered one that wants to be surprised by the game again, I suggest you give Better Planet Generation a try. It won't harm your game in any way.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629923717-282604290.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410307-2137550589.jpeg[/img]

[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=5]WARNING BEFORE INSTALLING :[/size][/b]

Installing this mod will operate changes to the terrain in every biomes.
It is also recommended to install it while in a space station / anomaly / freighter and not on a planet's surface.
Some major updates to the mod can reset the terrain or biome lists.
I'll include a warning on the download page and the sticky when it's the case.[/size][/size][/size][/size]


Recommended :
[/size][/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/968]DUD's Sky - Colors[/url] : load planetary colour palettes before BPG to avoid procedural textures conflicts.
[/size][/size]

name: Extrapolated Sunsets for Next
summary: Adds several new sunset themes, extrapolated from the day themes and the one existing sunset.
author: zagzag
description:
This mod adds to the existing one sunset by extrapolating from the existing daylight skies.

Currently, there are 40 different settings for daylight skies, and only one sunset. I took the difference of that one sunset with the first day sky (the most "normal" one), and applied it to every other sky, to create 39 more sunset colors. The result is fancy INTENSE sunsets.

Sadly, there is no actual correlation between what day sky the game chooses for each planet and what dusk sky, as I'd hoped, so a green sky does not necessarily fade into a green-generated sunset, but the variety is there.

I also have no way of knowing if the game actually uses the entire 40-long collection, or just from a few of the entries. But it appears to work.
[s]Additionally, I tweaked the sun and light colors for every day sky, to better match the sky color. The game has the same sun color for every sky, and only very slightly different light colors. However, it turns out this is almost imperceptible, and may be removed in the future, to eliminate the dependency on the dayskycolours file.  [/s] removed

The results are NOT slight, subtle or realistic in any way ( I don't think.. maybe if there are weird and exotic gasses in the atmosphere?) 
Sunsets don't last very long, though, and I thought a bit of gaudiness wouldn't be too bad. It remains to be seen if this turns out to be too tiring.

Incompatible with anything that modifies :

[s]- METADATA/SIMULATION/SOLARSYSTEM/WEATHER/SKYSETTINGS/DAYSKYCOLOURS.MBIN * [/s]removed
- METADATA/SIMULATION/SOLARSYSTEM/WEATHER/SKYSETTINGS/DUSKSKYCOLOURS.MBIN

name: AMUMSS (auto modbuilder-updater with MOD script definition system)
summary: Revolutionary No Man's Sky AMUMSS (auto modbuilder/updater with MOD script definition system)Fully automatic mod builder that automates every step of nms modding and provides an easy to use lua script mod definition system.
author: Mjjstral aka MetaIdea and Wbertro aka TheBossBoy
description:
[color=#ff0000][size=6]-> [/size][size=5]Looking for lua scripts, see:[/size][size=6] [url=https://github.com/MetaIdea/nms-amumss-lua-mod-script-collection]AMUMSS LUA MOD SCRIPT ARCHIVE[/url] <-[/size][/color]

[font=Verdana][color=#ffe599][size=6]AMUMSS is NOT a mod manager, nor can it be used as such.  It is a script processor.[/size][/color][/font]

[color=#ff0000][size=5]AMUMSS does NOT need updating to continue working when NMS updates.[/size]
[size=4]       When the tool MBINCompiler.exe is updated, AMUMSS will download it automatically.[/size][/color]
AMUMSS will auto-update itself (when allowed), if a new version is available (Just execute BUILDMOD.bat as usual and read simple instructions)
[list]
[*]The tools MBINCompiler/libMBIN are auto-updated by AMUMSS.[/*]
[*]Scripts are NEVER updated by AMUMSS, you are responsible for using updated scripts, when they need to be.[/*]
[/list]
[b][b][color=#ff7700][size=3]Questions will be answered if asked in NMS Discord[/size]: here[/color] [url=https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H][color=#00ff00]https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H[/color]
[/url][/b]
IMPORTANT NOTES:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]This tool works as long as MBINCompiler can compile/decompile the MBIN touched by the scripts[/b][/*]
[*][b]NEW version [color=#ff00ff]does[/color] requires [i][color=#ffff00].NET 6 Desktop Runtime x64[/color][/i] to be installed (a MBINCompiler.exe requirement)  It will attempt to auto-install.[/b][/*]
[*][b][color=#ff0000]Note:[/color] Sometimes, your anti-virus may detect some component of AMUMSS and block/quarantine it.  Be assure it is not a virus but its [i][color=#ff0000]behavior [/color][/i]may be interpreted as such by some anti-virus.  Please make sure to create an exception in your anti-virus when this happens.  Also a reboot may be required as some anti-virus do not correctly register the exception when created.[/b][/*]
[/list]
[b][i][size=3]INCLUDES:[/size][/i][/b]
[list]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]NMSPE [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]NMS PCBANKS Explorer)[/b][/u] a [color=#ff0000][b]GUI app to explore libMBIN.dll, NMS PAKS and Unpack/Decompile/Import-Search EXML content[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]AMM [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]_ModScript_Manager)[/b][/u] a [color=#ff0000][b]GUI app simplifying usage of scripts in ModScript folder[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]MFM [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]_MapFileMaker)[/b][/u]: a [color=#ff0000][b]NEW console app to create MapFileTree files from EXMLs using drag/drop[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[/list][b]
[b][color=#ff0000][size=4]Installation: [/size][/color][/b][/b]
[list]
[*][b][size=3][color=#00ff00]See [/color][color=#00ff00][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626][/url][/color][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626[/url]﻿ [color=#00ff00]for detailed instructions![/color][/size][/b]
[b]* [color=#ffff00]Get the latest release from[/color] [url=https://github.com/HolterPhylo/AMUMSS/releases]AMUMSS latest releases on Github Link
[/url]* De-compress the zip file to its own folder (like C:\AMUMSS)   Making the path to AMUMSS main folder short helps prevent bumping against windows path length limit (in rare cases)
* No accented or special characters in the path
* Always extract to a new folder on any drive, never in any System, Documents or game folder
* Create an exception to AMUMSS main folder BEFORE executing anything
[/b][color=#ffff00][b]* If the cmd windows closes immediately (or is blank) when double-clicking BUILDMOD.BAT:
          if you are on windows 7.  Try making '-UseColors N' in BUILDMOD_AUTO.bat and use it instead of BUILDMOD.bat

[/b][/color][/*]

name: Fast Actions 7.1
summary: Faster clicks and key pressed actions
author: Lo2k
description:
Instead of pressing a key for almost one second, this mod provides quicker or instant inputs for :
- menu selection
- creating/dismantling technology
- transfering resources to/from ship
- entering ship
- discussing with NPC
- gathering small plants
- launching refinery processing
- recovering deployable technologies

Enjoy !
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/28/?]
[/url]This mod exists in 2 versions :
﻿- instant actions : almost no delay actions (Fractal 4.13 added an in-game option but it only works for a limited range of actions)
- fast actions : between original and instant actions

[color=#ffd966]New : v7.1 fixes precise item quantity amount selection with Instant Actions. (Fast Action is untouched)[/color]


[size=2]Compatibility : [/size]

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/articles/85][color=#f6b26b]- [u]CHECK IF THIS MOD NEEDS TO BE MERGED WITH ANOTHER OF MY MODS[/u][/color][/url]

name: Exosolar's and Babs' Beyond Base Building
summary: ★ FULLY MP compatible, this mod adds an extreme degree of freedom to the vanilla base-building options
author: Exosolar Sparky NeptuneX3 Babscoole
description:
[b][color=#f1c232][size=4]What is this Mod?[/size][/color][/b]
[size=4]There are now almost 1,000 buildable base parts in vanilla No Man's Sky! It's a dream come true!
This mod adds an extreme degree of freedom to the vanilla base-building options.
This does NOT mean the mod gives you access to all buildable parts.  You still need to learn the blueprints for each.  :)
Beyond Base Building does NOT add any new build parts to the game.

Now includes all the functionality of gBase Items, [color=#ff0000]if chosen by running the Lua script[/color]:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]Can multiply power, extraction, and storage rates by 2, 5, or 10. WARNING: Bases built with this mod cannot be uploaded for multiplayer when using 2x, 5x, or 10x multiplied rates.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[list]
[*][size=4]EM Generators do not need a hotspot and produce 250 power.  Not enabled by default.  Must run Lua through ANUMSS and answer question "Choose a multiplier for Storage and Rates and EMAnywhere status" to generate custom mod file to enable.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]

It is [b]fully compatible[/b] with multiplayer, co-op building, uploading, etc, because it [i]does not add[/i] any parts, which would cause issues in multiplayer.[/size]

[size=4][color=#f1c232][b]★ Beyond Basebuilding[/b] is fully Lua scripted, with script provided for easy updating and merging [b]★[/b][/color] and carries over all of the official updates and improvements offered by Hello Games [b]plus[/b]:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]Most parts can be Scaled [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] prefab building parts, [color=#fff2cc]Exocraft Geobays, Mineral/Gas Extractors and Base Computer[/color] (Door attachment points bug out when scaled, and issues persist with scaled base computers and geobays).  There may be others not listed.  Be aware, scaling breaks snapping.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Many parts can be built in a Freighter.  [color=#fff2cc][i]Exceptions[/i][/color]  include the Base Computer, Industrial Mining parts, Vehicle Summoners and planet-based teleporter.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Many parts can be built at a Planetary Base [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] the freighter teleporter and the Orbital Exocraft Materialiser[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts can be built above and below water[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts can change color and material[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts, which can be deleted, can be deleted without also deleting attached objects [color=#fff2cc][i]except[/i][/color] Decals and Posters[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]No build-count limits [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] for parts related to resources harvesting, certain terminals, beacons and message capsules (and others with limits that are hard coded into the game, as they are added or changed by Hello Games).[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Unlimited Storage Containers[color=#cccccc][size=3][i]—*This does not increase actual storage amounts: Each numbered container acts as an index to the store rather than a physical box[/i][/size][/color][/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Foundation, Pavings and Communications Station can be placed anywhere (not only on bare terrain)[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Non corvette parts buildable with corvettes[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]
Some customization of the mod is available just by running it through AMUMSS, where you'll be prompted about enabling several feature.  Even more customization can be had by editing the top portion of the Lua before processing.  See Lua usage below for link to AMUMSS install and basic usage How to.[/size]

[color=#f1c232][b][u][size=5]Disclaimer:[/size][/u][/b][/color]
[size=3]This MOD makes changes to the placement and usage setting of vanilla parts.  If the MOD is removed, the vanilla settings will be restored and parts that "violate" a vanilla setting as well as those connected may go "poof".  Best practice is to regularly backup your saves for semi-painless recovery from the unexpected.[/size]

[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]

[size=5][b][u][color=#f1c232]Installation[/color]
[/u][/b][/size]
[b]Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS

[color=#f1c232][size=4][u]Lua usage[/u][/size][/color]
[size=4][b]✦[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]How to use AMUMSS at No Man's Sky Nexus - Mods and Community (nexusmods.com)[/url]✦[/b][/size][/b]

[size=4][u][b][color=#f1c232]Modified Files[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[color=#d8d8d8][size=3]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\BASEBUILDINGOBJECTSTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color]

[i][color=#cccccc][size=3]Also check out...[/size][/color][/i]
[size=5]✦[b][color=#ffdd00][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/58018181?tab=user+files]Exosolar's Other Mods[/url][/color][/b]✦[/size]


[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]

[size=5][u][b][color=#f1c232]Is this Mod up-to-date?[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[size=4][i][b]Any mod files which are not hidden or demoted to the "Old Files" area can be expected to work.[/b][/i]
All uploaded files (on the Files page) are maintained with their packing date and version of the MBINCompiler used to pack it. Older mod versions, no longer compatible with the current version of No Man's Sky will be demoted to the "Old Files" subsection, but will remain available for download. There are no side-effects to using this mod.[/size]

[size=4][u][b][color=#f1c232]How to update, tweak, combine, or patch the mod with AMUMSS?[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[size=4]As this mod is now Lua scripted, you can [color=#fff2cc]easily [b]update[/b], [b]tweak[/b] or [b]merge[/b] the mod yourself[/color] (if a game update breaks this PAK of the mod, or you have other PAK or Lua mods that could conflict with this one). [color=#cccccc][size=3][i]—*Click on the spoiler below for more[/i][/size][/color][/size]
[spoiler]
[size=3][u]Notes on AMUMSS:[/u]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url] tries to update [url=https://github.com/monkeyman192/MBINCompiler/releases]MBINcompiler[/url] automatically. If it fails to convert EXML/MBIN files, its likely due to a very recent NMS version update and MBINcompiler is awaiting an update too.
If so, I highly recommend you to disable your mods and wait for MBINCompiler to be updated (it's pretty quick in general).[/size]


[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Updating the mod after a game update:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][list]
[*] [size=3]Download [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url], and extract it locally,  avoid placing the AMUMSS folder in a path with special characters or in a system folder like [color=#fff2cc][i]"C:\Program Files (x86)\(...)"[/i][/color].[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Place the mod Lua script (only the .LUA file, not the .PAK file) in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]AMUMSS will normally auto-detect the NMS installation folder, but you can also manually edit the path in the [color=#fff2cc][i]NMS_FOLDER.txt[/i][/color] file.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root of the AMUMSS folder and follow the steps, selecting the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]A new modded PAK file will be created, using the last update of the MBIN game files duly amended by the script.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=3]Most of the time simply recompiling the mod using the Lua script will make it compatible again. Use the latest script to ensure you adopt any mod adjustments to accommodate the latest NMS build.[/size][size=3] There are two main causes of mods not correctly updating with this method:
1) MBINCompiler needs an update to be able to decompile the latest version of the game MBIN files.
2) Structural changes have been made to the MBIN files called by this Lua script. Whilst unlikely, this will require some script rewrite and other mods are likely affected.[/size][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Tweaking the mod:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]Use the same process as described in [color=#fff2cc][i]Updating the mod[/i][/color].
Just modify the script as you want before recompiling it. Look at the top of my scripts, you can generally edit modifiers there, or even disable/enable some features very easily.[/size]

[size=3][color=#cccccc][i]*[url=https://notepad-plus-plus.org/]Notepad++[/url] or [url=https://code.visualstudio.com/download/]VSCode[/url] are generally recommended to read EXML and Lua files, due to large file support and automatic highlighting. The [url=https://sourceforge.net/projects/npp-compare/]Compare plugin[/url] for Notepad++, and Lua is also a must-have to highlight file differences.[/i][/color][/size][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Combining mods that provide Lua scripts:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]You'll want to do this if multiple mods affect the same MBIN file.[/size]

[list]
[*] [size=3]Place all the scripts in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Follow the step, selecting that you want to combine all the scripts into one PAK file, and the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=3]AMUMSS will output a PAK file that will contain the modifications of all the scripts.[/size]
[color=#cccccc][size=3][i]*If the scripts modify the exact same values in the MBIN file, the last loaded script will overwrite the preceding script values.[/i][/size][/color][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Patch in the mod to make it compatible with other PAK files:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]You will want to do this if another mods isn't Lua scripted, and affects the same MBIN file as the Lua scripted mod.

It will work when :
Case 1) you patch a Lua scripted mod for only one other PAK.
Case 2) you patch a Lua scripted mod for several other PAKs, but only if the other PAKs don't modify the same MBIN files between them.[/size]

[size=3][b]How to create a patched PAK:[/b][/size]

[list]
[*] [size=3]Copy the script and the other mod(s) PAK(s) in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root folder of AMUMSS, and follow the steps, selecting the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]The new PAK file will contain all the Lua script modifications, and a compatibility patch for the other mod(s) PAK(s).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Rename it something like [color=#fff2cc][i]"__zzz Scripted Mod Name -Patched For- The Other Mod Name.pak"[/i][/color].[/size]
[size=3][color=#fff2cc][i]"__zzz"[/i][/color] prefix is to ensure that it'll be loaded after the mod(s) it was patched for (for NMS loading order, underscore [i]"_"[/i] came after every other characters).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Remove the original scripted mod PAK from the [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder, and copy both the new patched one, and the other mod(s) PAK(s), into it.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=3][u]Notes:[/u]
[b]Be sure the mod PAK you want to create a compatibility patch for, is updated for the latest NMS version.[/b]
-> When AMUSS find an MBIN file affected by a script and by a PAK in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder, it will take the MBIN file from the mod PAK over the original one of the game, and will apply to it the modifications listed in the script.
So, if the MBIN file of the mod PAK is not updated, the one in the resulting patched PAK won't be either.[/size][/spoiler]
[/spoiler]

[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]
[color=#f1c232][u][b][size=5]Philosophy[/size][/b][/u][/color]
[size=4]All the erroneous limitations in building are pretty silly, aren't they? In making this mod, I paid specific attention to making sure I did not make changes that will invalidate the bases built with it from online play. Any base made with Beyond BaseBuilding will be fully viable and visible in online play, even when other players don't have this mod! It does this by only editing placement rules while you are in the building editor and not modifying any of the actual base parts themselves.
We hope you enjoy the mod and naturally, your comments are most welcome. We can also be found on the No Man's Sky Discord or reached directly at: [color=#f1c232]Babscoole #6326 [/color]or [color=#f1c232]Exosolar#5120[/color], or if its about the Lua script: [color=#f1c232]NeptuneX3#8095[/color] [size=3][i][size=4]or [/size][/i][/size][color=#f1c232]s p a r k y#0536[/color][/size]


[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]

And, of course, thanks to all of the other modders who make mods too, as we often inspect the work of other modders to learn how things are done: [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/12249518]RangerDulann[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/44146502]Redmas[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/1964784]WoodyMontana[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/42286415]jasondude[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/users/38106355?tab=user+files]Gumsk[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/45932167?tab=user+files]Mjjstral[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/41291360]moddinaccount[/url], Devilin Pixy, saa044, and others have all helped us learn to mod effectively! Without the above-mentioned, their mods and applications, we would be unable to make any mods at all![/i][/size]

name: Exosolar's and Babs' Planet Type Converters
summary: Transform half of all planets into the type of your choice with one of these "Planet Type Converters"!
author: Exosolar and Babscoole
description:
[b][size=5][u]What is this Mod?[/u][/size]
[/b][size=4]This mod takes half of all planets in every system, and causes that half to become the planet type of your choice as long as the mod is installed. There are 13 options:

﻿- Barren
﻿- Dead
﻿- Exotic
﻿- Frozen
﻿- Lush
﻿- Radioactive
﻿- Scorched
﻿- Toxic
﻿- Weird
﻿- Swamp
﻿- Lava
      - Gas Giants
      - Waterworlds

Excellent for casual control of your game experience or for forcing an abundance of specific planets to test your own mods. :)

Any planets converted by the Planet Type Converter will be reverted when the converter is uninstalled.... and converted back again if re-installed.  This change is client side.

In the .zip, there are several different packs, each with a name that indicates a planet type. Whichever planet type you want to see tons of, use the .pak with the name of that planet type.  Use only one at a time.
[/size]
[b][u][size=5]Philosophy[/size][/u]
[/b][size=4]Personally I just wanted to test mods, but some others expressed a lack of time or will to go sifting for hours or days to find certain kinds of planets or may have found themselves in a problematic position during the early game.[/size]

[u][b][size=5]Known Side-Effects[/size]
[/b][/u][size=4]This mod has the power to alter the planetary types of online mission planets, particularly the weekend missions. The mod should be temporarily uninstalled when doing missions which require specific planet types to appear normally.

When a planet that has been previously explored is forcibly changed to another type, discovery data will be disrupted. Flora, Fauna, and minerals on the planet may change and previous discovery data on them will be overwritten.
[/size]
[u][size=5][b]Modified Files[/b][/size][/u][size=4][u][b]
[/b][/u]METADATA\SIMULATION\SOLARSYSTEM\BIOMES\BIOMELISTPERSTARTYPE

[size=5]✦[b][color=#ffdd00][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/58018181?tab=user+files]Exosolar's Other Mods[/url][/color][/b]✦[/size][/size]

[size=5][u][b]About Available Versions
[/b][/u][/size][size=4]MBINCompiler version used will match the NMS and MOD versions. Any older mod that is no longer compatible with the current version of No Man's Sky will be demoted to the "Old Files" subsection, but will remain available for download. If I have missed one, please tell me.

[i][b]This means that any mod files which are not hidden or demoted to the "Old Files" area can be expected to work.
[/b][/i]
MBINCompiler versions exactly match the version of No Man's Sky that was current when the MBINCompiler was made. In other words, MBINCompiler version = NMS version. This fact can be used to figure out which Legacy mods work with which Legacy versions of No Man's Sky.[size=4]

[/size][b][u][size=5]Installation[/size]
[/u][/b][size=3]
[/size]Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS
[/size]
[size=5][u][b]Credits and Special Thanks![/b][/u][/size][u][b][size=4]
[/size][/b][/u][size=4]Hello Games for making the wonderful, vast, and fun game, No Man's Sky!
monkeyman192 for the MBINCompiler and his continued efforts to keep it up-to-date
Tub0Crisco for the No Man's Sky Mod Station A.K.A. NMSMS

[b]Big thanks[/b] to any and all of you lovely people who offer donations! It is greatly appreciated, and will help me buy lunch while I'm busy being a broke college student! :D[/size][size=4]

[/size][size=4]And, of course, all of the other modders who make mods too! name: Better Freighters 1.1
summary: This mod allows for your fleet to actually defend you should you come into a situation that requires it.  In the base game, freighters will shoot at the occasional pirate, and may even score a few hits, however they did no damage whatsoever (kind of pathetic when you look at the size of the guns).
author: Kiecam
description:
This mod allows for your fleet to actually defend you should you come into a situation that requires it.  In the base game, freighters will shoot at the occasional pirate, and may even score a few hits, however they did no damage whatsoever (kind of pathetic when you look at the size of the guns). This mod enables freighters to actually damage enemy ships, and all of the guns on your freighter will open up on its nearest target as long as they have scored any hits on your freighter (if you are out in the middle of space and enemy ships don't miss you and hit your freighter it will not shoot back this is a WIP and how the base game functions).  This mod will more than likely be further expanded to include adding proper damage numbers to trader ships who may want to help you fight as they also do no damage.

Update 1.1: Freighters now have better accuracy, shoot continuously without having a max number of shots before disengaging and will continue to track the target if it goes out of visibility (before the freighter guns would lose interest completely as soon as it went out of range.  This wouldn't reset until the freighter came out of, and went back into combat mode.

name: Better Rewards
summary: Increase the amount of currency you get with faster word learning and increased resource gathering options. Frigate (Freighter) Expeditions are also included!
author: MrTrack
description:
Better Rewards increases the amount of units, nanite clusters and quicksilver you get from completing missions or interacting with objects in the world. Non npc word learning (such as knowledge stones and dictionaries) is also faster.

[color=#bf9000][size=3]IMPORTANT NOTE: Don't use this mod when I haven't updated it to the new game version (especially when it is a new or returning expedition)
[/size][/color][color=#bf9000]if you really want to use it when I haven't updated it yet, make sure you [/color][color=#bf9000]back up your save[/color][color=#bf9000] or build the mod using the lua scripts yourself.[/color]

[b]Currency[/b]

Currency gain is roughly increased by 5 times, so as to make it a viable option besides crafting and selling valuable trading goods. Some unit values were tremendously increased (eg.: from minimum amount of 1000-2000 to 20000-50000) like the terminals in abandoned settlements to make the early game less grindy.

Includes:
[list]
[*]Space station missions and Nexus missions
[*]Interactable objects/devices (abandoned buildings, damaged machinery, waypoints, etc.)
[*]Sentinels
[*]Alien interactions
[/list]
[b]Words[/b]

Learning words from non npc interactions (knowledge stones and dictionaries) is also 3 times faster (3 words learned form a single knowledge stone). You now also learn 5 Atlas words, instead of 1 and also have a chance (15%) to learn them when interacting with non npc learning sources.

[b]Resources

[/b]When gathering with the mining beam you generally get 2 times more resources (but only when the things explode as you mine them). Also includes containers, depots, guild rewards, asteroids and ships that drop resources.

[b]Frigate (Freighter) Expedition Rewards

[/b]After a successful expedition, when collecting your rewards you get 5x more units and nanites, 2x more products like Storage Augmentation and NipNip Buds and 3x more resources like Copper, Emeril and Cadmium.
[b]
Installation

[/b]Make sure you delete or edit DISABLEMODS.TXT in your game library (...\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS) before trying to use mods. Extract the zip file to ...\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder and you are good to go (Create MODS folder if you don't have one). Alternatively you can just use Vortex to download and install.

This mod modifies the [color=#ff0000][size=3]REWARDTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color] and [color=#ff0000][size=3]EXPEDITIONREWARDTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color] file and will not work with other mods that use those tables.

[b]Updated to NMS version 6.21 (Build ID: 21918502) on 2026.02.13.[/b]

name: Increased S class starship and freighter spawn chances
summary: This mod increases the chance for starships as well as freighters to spawn as S class in systems they are able to spawn, to reduce the amount of grind to find them.
author: Skip
description:
Other mods that do the same thing are all outdated and don't include lua scripts. This one does, so you can recompile it yourself whenever the game updates, if you need to.

Chances for S class is now 5% in average systems and 10% in wealthy systems. C class ships are less common as a result.

I'm not sure if multitools are also affected, but I couldn't find any data on them so far. Let me know in the comments if you find more information.

name: No Man's Sky RayTracing Shader
summary: Path Tracing, Global Illumination, Enhanced Ambient Occlusion, Screen Space Reflections, Depth of Field (toggle by "DELETE/DEL" Key), Color Correction, Fog/Haze, and Back Face Illumination.
author: Pascal Gilcher
description:
This [b][size=4]REQUIRES [/size][/b]the RTGI shader made by [u][b]Pascal Gilcher[/b][/u].
[quote][size=6]﻿[b]﻿ITS IS NOT FREE (but its quite affordable)[/b][/size][/quote]
You can visit his patreon here: https://www.patreon.com/mcflypg/posts and obtain the files by pledging to him.

This is just my ReShade preset file using the RTGI shader along with qUNIT shaders. This preset does have path tracing and screen space reflections along with enhanced ambient occlusion and depth of field mixed with color correction and back face illumination AND global illumination. Hope you enjoy!

NOTE - This preset is HEAVY so low end users beware (If you run the base game on low setting at 10fps don't use this)

Used on an RTX 3070 and average frames range from 40 to 70+ FPS with game settings on ultra and everything maxed including DLAA.

"ReShade with full Add-On Support" version is required to have access to the depth buffer since it is required for this to work.

It can be found here: https://reshade.me/?ref=vgtimes

files.name: Ceph's RTGI ReShade Preset
files.description: My ReShade preset config file for PathTracing on the RTGI Shader

name: Ship Interior Procedural Color
summary: Restores procedural colors for the cockpit interior plastic parts; The mod affects all types except the living ship.
author: lMonk
description:
[size=3][color=#BF9000][size=5][b]Ship Interior Procedural Color 1.14[/b][/size][/color]

[color=#BF9000][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/color]
I remember we used to have some color variation inside the cockpit, but somewhere in the long line of updates it vanished, leaving us with permanent red paneling and controls... boring.
This mod restores procedural colors for the cockpit interior plastic parts; It affects all types except the living ship.

⚠️ Overwrite mod - unlikely to work well with other Overwrite mods

[color=#BF9000][size=4][b]Install[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Unzip the mod and place the full folder into [color=#f4cccc]{NMS Install Folder}\GAMEDATA\MODS[/color] (not PCBANKS). Create MODS folder if necessary.
[size=3]If the mod(s) still doesn't work, delete the mod settings file [color=#f4cccc]{NMS Install Folder}\Binaries\SETTINGS\GCMODSETTINGS.MXML[/color] and try again.[/size][/size]
◈ The LUA isn't used by the game directly. It's an aid for merging with other mods, using AMUMSS, or modding yourself. If you don't do either, you can safely ignore it.

[/size][size=4][b][color=#bf9000]Change-log[/color][/b][/size][size=3]
1.14: Synced with game version [b]6.21[/b]

[color=#BF9000][size=4][b]Compatibility[/b][/size][/color]
New files only.[/size]

name: DPPBFOYA (Sentinel Spawns Multiplier)
summary: Multiply Sentinel Spawns
author: WinderTP and lyravega
description:
[center][size=6][b][u]﻿DA PO PO BE FAST ON YO ASS


[/u][/b][/size][/center][center]﻿
[youtube]99smSafeFEo[/youtube]
[youtube]q97cQYWaNUM[/youtube][/center]
[size=6][b][u][center]DPPBFOYA is not designed to be balanced,
and is recommended to have at least a 4-player team
(with component squadrons for space battles) to fight.[/center][/u][/b][/size]

Da Po Po Be Fast On Yo Ass (DPPBFOYA) multiplies existing Sentinel spawn numbers in the wild and wanted waves, including:
[list=1]
[*]All [b][url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Drone]Wandering Drones[/url] [/b]([i]PatrolDrone[/i]) minimum multiplied by 2, maximum by 10;
[*]All [b][url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Heavy_Combat_Drone]Heavy Combat Drones[/url][/b] ([i]CombatDrone[/i]) minimum multiplied by 2, maximum by 7.5;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Repair_Drone]Repair Drones[/url] ([i]MedicDrone[/i]) minimum multiplied by 2, maximum by 4;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Summoner_Drone]Summoner Drones[/url] ([i]SummonerDrone[/i]) maximum multiplied by 2.5;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Quad]Quads[/url] ([i]Quad[/i]) minimum multiplied by 1.5, maximum by 4.5;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Hardframe_Battle_Mech]Hardframe Battle Mechs[/url] ([i]Mech[/i]) maximum multiplied by 2;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Walker]Walkers[/url] ([i]Walker[/i]) maximum multiplied by 2;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel]Corrupted Drones[/url]﻿ ([i]CorruptedDrone[/i]) minimum multiplied by 1.5, maximum by 7.5;
[*]All [url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Sentinel_Starship_Fighter_Drone]Starship Fighter Drones[/url]﻿ ([i]Standard [/i]under SHIP_MULTIPLIER) in wanted waves minimum by 2.5, maximum by 6.5
[/list]
[size=5][b]Optional Space Station Starship Fighter Drones multiplication
[/b][/size]
In the AMUMSS lua script is the optional multiplication to Starship Fighter Drones. It is not enabled in the main file download since it edits a global (frequently modified) file which can increase mod conflicts.

To enable it, simply deploy the lua script with AMUMSS, it defaults to [i]ON[/i], to disable it in AMUMSS, allow user input from scripts in AMUMSS, and input "N" when asked (highlighted red below):

[img]https://i.imgur.com/Kve9Ifj.png[/img]

[u]DPPBFOYA does not increase the chances of wandering Sentinel spawns, only the number of Sentinels spawned in the aforementioned groups.[/u]


This mod can be customised to change the multipliers for all types of Sentinels with the use of the lua AMUMSS script:
[list]
[*][i]Type[/i] indicates the type of Sentinel the below 2 parameters will multiply;
[*][i]MinAmount [/i]indicates the multiplication of the minimum amount of spawns;
[*][i]MaxAmount[/i][i] [/i]indicates the multiplication of the maximum amount of spawns;
[/list]
[center][url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Mods#Installing_Mods][size=6]Installing Mods Manually[/size][/url][/center][size=6][center][url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky:Tutorials/HowToAMUMSS]How To merge with AMUMSS[/url]
[url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky:Tutorials/Merging_two_mods]How to merge without AMUMSS[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]How to use AMUMSS (pdf)[/url][/center][/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957][size=5][b][center]Click here to go to AMUMSS's Nexus mod page.[/center][/b][/size][/url]
[u][size=4][b]ALL EXML files are configured to be maximally compatible with other mods and require no merging via AMUMSS[/b][/size][/u]
Edits METADATA\SIMULATION\SCENE\EXPERIENCESPAWNTABLE.MBIN
﻿GLOBALS\GCAISPACESHIPGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN

The lowest-level frigate LOD model tends to pop-up when the freighter is parked in orbit above a planet. This mod fixes the issue.

⚠️ Overwrite mod - unlikely to work well with other Overwrite mods

[size=3]Unzip the mod and place the full folder into [color=#f4cccc]{NMS Install Folder}\GAMEDATA\MODS[/color] (not PCBANKS). Create MODS folder if necessary.

name: WEED-B-GONE
summary: This mod prevents all four varieties of hazardous plant from spawning in any biome, anywhere.
author: Verteiron
description:
[size=5]Updates[/size]

Version 0.9.8: Adds support for [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/3405]Mod Catalogue Framework[/url] - as a contributor to SkyUI it'd be pretty hypocritical of me to not support any attempt to organize mods in-game.
Version 0.9.7: Fixes a filename bug on the spikey plant that allowed them to start spawning again in 0.9.5. Thanks to @[url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/JMZawodny?gameId=1634]JMZawodny[/url] for reporting the issue so I could get it fixed quickly!
Version 0.9.5: Also removes the horrible gassy cave "plants"!

[size=5]Also check out No Hazardous Plants 2.0![/size]

Between the time I started looking into this and the time I published it, No Hazardous Plants was revived and extended by Lo2k over at [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2682]No Hazardous Plants 2.0[/url]﻿! Lo2k's approach appears to be similar to cruzdroid's original method but updated for the current version of the game.

The short version:
[list]
[*]WEED-B-GONE removes all hazardous plants everywhere by deliberately breaking their in-game model. This is simple, appears to work with minimal fuss or changes to existing saves, and removed ALL instances of the plants, including those inside abandoned structures, etc. It should only need updating if more hazardous flora are added to the game.
[*]NHP2 removes hazardous plants by updating the game's XML files. This means that more files need to be changed, but it also means it's not relying on a fallback behavior, which is probably safer. Some hazardous plants may remain in POIs, and this method will need updating if new biomes or POIs are added in future game releases.
[/list]
Both mods do what they say on the label, but our approaches are completely different, so take your pick!

[size=5]Intro[/size]

I... [i]hate [/i]the hazardous plants.

They are on my planets, cluttering up the scenery.

They are outside my base walls, hitting me.

They are inside my base.

They are inside my head, and I am crazy.

No more. [size=5]No more.[/size] [size=6]NO. MORE. PLANTS.[/size]

I have eradicated them. The galaxy is finally, finally... [i]clean[/i].

You want oxygen? Go diving for kelp. No water? Well, it's not like it's a [i]habitable [/i]planet anyway. Bring your air with you.
[size=5]
Details[/size]

Okay, now that [i]that's[/i] out my system: this mod completely removes (as far as I can tell) the following entities from the game:

[list]
[*]SporeVent plants (the big green noisy gassy ones that ruin everything, everywhere)
[*]Tentacle plants (the ones that whip you and hiss through your base walls)
[*]Flytrap plants (the ones that bite you from under your base floor)
[*]Spike plants (the newer ones that look kind of like a sundew, which also whip you through your base walls)
[*][b]Update[/b]: The weird little gassy puffballs that have been inside caves since release (v 0.9.5 and up)
[/list]
They will not spawn anywhere, in any biome, and any that are already present in your save will vanish.

[size=5]Background[/size][size=4]

[/size]As mentioned above, I'm not a huge fan of the hazardous flora, particularly the green gas "puffers", or the "SporeVent" plants, as the game calls them internally. It's that squeaky, rubbery balloon noise that gets me, and with the recent updates it seems like there's literally nowhere I can escape them. They spawn in groups of 3-4 at a time usually, and they're everywhere, and we HATES them, precious.

Alas, it seems the technique used by cruzdroid's [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2357]ImmersiveGen[/url]﻿ to suppress them doesn't really work any longer, so I went digging around and here's what I found:

[list]
[*]All the hazardous plants in the game have multiple paths for spawning.
[*]The original three plants appear to be fallback possibilities for other kinds of spawns that won't fit in a given area.
[*]The spore-vent plants appear to have 2 additional methods for appearing, some of which they share with the others, which is why you often get 2-3 of them at a time in clumps.
[/list]
The practical upshot of all this is that (as lots of us have noticed) the hazardous plants are completely out of control, and just editing their own MXML files doesn't seem to be enough to get rid of them anymore. I have to edit three different files in order to get rid of them via EXML settings, and even then, they're written right into the individual Biome data, so I'd have to edit EVERY SINGLE BIOME in order to completely eradicate them.

I did discover ways to reduce the spawn rates (and do other funny things like make them all tiny), but it still involved making changes to key
shared files in ways that might not play nice with other mods.

So I tried just replacing their scene files with invalid copies to see if the game would fail to load them properly and (hopefully) ignore them.

As it turns out, that works great, and means there's basically zero chance of conflicting with or overwriting another mod's changes. This is probably about as clean as it gets.

I tried backing it up one level and exclusively modifying the MXML files for their PLANT entries, but it seems like the game doesn't pay attention to those anymore, or at least has other methods for reading out the vanilla hazards and placing them. Only replacing the SCENE files themselves seems to have the desired effect.

[size=5]Known Bugs[/size]
[list]
[*]NPCs may still give you quests involving Hazardous Flora, but they will be impossible to complete. [b]If you want to re-enable one plant type for doing quests or collecting oxygen, I recommend deleting the "HAZARDPLANTSPIKEY.SCENE.MBIN" file from the mod's folder.[/b] The newer spiked-sundew plants seem to have the lowest spawn rate and they're at least quiet when they're not attacking you.
[*]Since hazardous plants no longer count toward your scan totals, I don't believe this will affect existing flora counts. I've also not observed any issues with other spawns getting moved or changed as a result of using this mod. However, it is possible that some plant or random item placements will be altered.
[/list]
[size=5]Thanks to[/size][list]
[*][url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/cruzdroid]Cruzdroid[/url]﻿ for the original [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1898]No Hazardous Flora[/url]﻿﻿ and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2357]ImmersiveGen[/url]﻿, which pointed me at the right files to clear out
[*][url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Lo2k/mods?gameId=1634]Lo2k[/url]﻿ for coming up with the updated [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2682]No Hazardous Plants 2.0[/url]﻿ at the same time I decided I was so sick of them I'd learn how to mod the game to get rid of them.
[*][url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/JMZawodny?gameId=1634]JMZawodny[/url]﻿ for letting me know that I broke the spikey plant's filename in an update. Don't update your mods while under the influence, kids!
[/list]
[size=5]Files modified[/size]

MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\INTERACTIVE\SPOREVENT.SCENE.MBIN
MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\INTERACTIVEFLORA\HAZARDPLANTSPIKEY.SCENE.MBIN
MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\INTERACTIVEFLORA\TENTACLEPLANT.SCENE.MBIN

These are 0-byte files that are placed in order to keep the game from loading the asset for their respective plants. If you want to restore one of the plants to its vanilla spawn state, DELETE its respective SCENE.MBIN file from the mod's folder.

name: Dismantle Starship Reactors
summary: Reduce - reuse - recycle
author: FriendlyFirePL
description:
[size=5][b]Description[/b][/size]

This mod allows players to scrap the various starship reactor cores that can be used to fabricate a ship into usuable materials reflecting the equivalent tiers of hyperdrive blueprints. So ex. scrapping an A-class reactor module will give player half the materials used for construction of Emeril Drive: 120x Cadmium + 2x Wiring Loom. The new option is available in player inventory by hovering on the reactor module item and presing the key for "Dismantle" option.

The idea behind this mod is to provide extra functionality for these items for early-game players (who can't assemble a full ship but struggle with specific resources), as well as late-game players (who already own a personalized starship fleet and have no use for these items).

[line]

[b][size=5]Technical information[/size][/b]

[quote][b]Files modified by this mod:[/b]

METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\CONSUMABLEITEMTABLE.MBIN
METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\REWARDTABLE.MBIN[/quote]

[quote][b]Mod specification:[/b]

✅ EXML patch mod

This mod is likely to work with other mods modifying the same file.[/quote]

[line]

[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]

[quote][b]Installation with ZIP archive (manual / mod manager)[/b]

Download the provided ZIP archive and extract its contents into game's mods directory: [b]GAMEDATA\MODS[/b]

(if the MODS folder doesn't exist, create it yourself)

After extracting check that the GAMEDATA\MODS directory contains the dedicated folder for this mod (FF_ModName_ModVersion), not just individual files / folders (ex. TEXTURES, MODELS, METADATA, etc.).

If you experience problems with installing mods, please refer to the [url=http://tinyurl.com/nmsmodding550]NMS Modding After 5.50 article[/url] by Gumsk.[/quote]

name: Remove Corvette Module Tech
summary: Removes the stat upgrade techs created by various corvette modules, making them entirely cosmetic.  Optional files available to remove only specific types of module.
author: LucraisJager
description:
Removes the various randomized stat bonus technologies created in the ship technology inventory by corvette modules.

[b]Warning: Installing the full mod, habitat or cargo options will cause your corvettes to spawn with fewer cargo slots, deleting items or locking them in the corvette modules cache.  Make backups, install with caution if your holds are full.
[/b]
The weapon technologies cannot be removed as without these the game doesn't know where corvette turrets are mounted and will fire from a nearby random point in space.

Alternate files are included to remove only specific types of modules, for modularity and to avoid mod conflicts.

A corvette docked on a space station pad will process these changes immediately.  Corvettes saved in other locations may take a while to react.
